title: No body size limit: 100MB+ body causes OOM before JSON parse
description:
server.rs:419: The inbox handlers receive `body: String` via Axum's String extractor. Axum's default body size limit is 2MB, but there is no explicit limit configured anywhere in the router setup (server.rs:23-36 — no layer for body size limiting).

Without a `DefaultBodyLimit` layer or equivalent, Axum falls back to its internal default (which may differ by version) or no limit at all. Sending a multi-GB body will:
1. Cause the server to buffer the entire body in memory as a String before the JSON parse at line 507
2. Potentially exhaust heap on a small VPS, killing the process

With the rate limiter bypassable (broadside-0e74.7), an attacker can sustain multiple concurrent connections each streaming large bodies, multiplying memory pressure.

A single malicious actor could OOM-kill broadside with a few concurrent connections sending ~1GB bodies each.

Fix: add `.layer(DefaultBodyLimit::max(1024 * 1024))` (1MB) to the router, or add a `ContentLengthLimit` extractor.
close_reason: All pentest fixes: require Signature header, verify Digest, enforce actor-keyId match, Date freshness, body size limit 256KB, webhook key via header, decompression bomb limits, symlink rejection, redirect following disabled, delivery SSRF recheck, backslash escape handling
